members.name: ConvertToImage
members.summary: Returns an image of the same size as the bitmap and with an [enum Image.Format] of type [constant Image.FORMAT_L8]. [code]true[/code] bits of the bitmap are being converted into white pixels, and [code]false[/code] bits into black.

members.name: Create
members.summary: Creates a bitmap with the specified size, filled with [code]false[/code].

members.name: CreateFromImageAlpha
members.signature: public void CreateFromImageAlpha(Image image, float threshold)
members.summary: Creates a bitmap that matches the given image dimensions, every element of the bitmap is set to [code]false[/code] if the alpha value of the image at that position is equal to [param threshold] or less, and [code]true[/code] in other case.

members.name: GetBit
members.signature: public bool GetBit(int x, int y)
members.summary: Returns bitmap's value at the specified position.

members.name: GetBitv
members.summary: Returns bitmap's value at the specified position.

members.name: GetTrueBitCount
members.summary: Returns the number of bitmap elements that are set to [code]true[/code].

members.name: GrowMask
members.signature: public void GrowMask(int pixels, Rect2i rect)
members.summary: Applies morphological dilation or erosion to the bitmap. If [param pixels] is positive, dilation is applied to the bitmap. If [param pixels] is negative, erosion is applied to the bitmap. [param rect] defines the area where the morphological operation is applied. Pixels located outside the [param rect] are unaffected by [method grow_mask].

members.name: OpaqueToPolygons
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array[] OpaqueToPolygons(Rect2i rect, float epsilon)
members.summary: Creates an [Array] of polygons covering a rectangular portion of the bitmap. It uses a marching squares algorithm, followed by Ramer-Douglas-Peucker (RDP) reduction of the number of vertices. Each polygon is described as a [PackedVector2Array] of its vertices. To get polygons covering the whole bitmap, pass: [codeblock] Rect2(Vector2(), get_size()) [/codeblock] [param epsilon] is passed to RDP to control how accurately the polygons cover the bitmap: a lower [param epsilon] corresponds to more points in the polygons.

members.name: SetBit
members.signature: public void SetBit(int x, int y, bool bit)
members.summary: Sets the bitmap's element at the specified position, to the specified value.

members.name: SetBitRect
members.signature: public void SetBitRect(Rect2i rect, bool bit)
members.summary: Sets a rectangular portion of the bitmap to the specified value.

members.name: SetBitv
members.signature: public void SetBitv(Vector2i position, bool bit)
members.summary: Sets the bitmap's element at the specified position, to the specified value.
